editorial.title: 하늘이 자신을 살렸다는 이 대통령
editorial.body: (사설) 이재명 대통령이 검찰 조작기소, 언론명예살인, 피습 헬기 이송 논란 등 3대 위협을 언급하며 국민이 구했다고 소셜미디어에 밝혀 논란이 되고 있습니다. | 이재명 대통령은 엑스에 자신이 검찰과 언론과 테러 위협에서 살아났다고 밝혔습니다.,그는 권익위가 2024년 부산 피습 뒤 헬기 이송 판단을 번복한 기사를 공유했습니다.,사설은 권익위 판단이 정권에 따라 바뀌는 점과 공소 취소 논의가 사법을 흔든다고 비판했습니다.
summary: 이재명 대통령이 검찰·언론·테러 위협에서 '국민이 구했다'고 주장해 논란
권익위 헬기 이송 판단 번복으로 정권에 따른 자의성 지적
사설, 공소 취소 논의가 사법부를 흔든다고 비판

editorial.titleTr: Heaven Saved Him, Says This President
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung claims 'the people saved' him from prosecution, media, and terror threats, stirring controversy
Anti-Corruption Agency reverses helicopter transfer decision, raising concerns about arbitrary judgment based on political administration
Editorial: Discussion of dropping charges is shaking the judiciary
editorial.bodyTr: An editorial has sparked controversy after President Lee Jae-myung posted on social media that the public saved him from three major threats: prosecutorial manipulation of charges, media defamation, and controversy over helicopter transport following an attack. President Lee Jae-myung stated on X that he survived threats from the prosecution, the media, and terrorism. He shared an article about how the Anti-Corruption and Civil Rights Commission reversed its decision on helicopter transport following the attack in Busan in 2024. The editorial criticized how the Anti-Corruption and Civil Rights Commission's judgment changes depending on the administration and how discussions about dropping charges shake the judicial system.

editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 조정식(왼쪽부터)·김태년·박지원 의원이 국회의장 출마 기자회견을 하고 있다. 연합뉴스 향후 2년 동안 제22대 국회 후반기를 이끌어갈 국회의장 후보 자리에 더불어민주당 조정식(6선), 박지원(5선), 김태년(5선) 의원이 도전장을 냈다. 당 소속 의원 투표로 국회의장 후보를 뽑아 온 민주당은 이번부터 의원 투표 비율을 80%로 줄이고, 당원 투표를 20% 반영한다. 민주당이 과반 의석을 차지한 만큼 민주당 경선에서 이기면 사실상 국회의장이 된다. 어제부터 당원 투표가 실시 중인데, 세 후보 모두 이재명 정부 지원을 앞세우느라 중립적 국회 운영이나 야당과의 협치는 뒷전이다. 강성 당원의 구미에 맞추려고 국회의장의 직분을 망각한 처사가 아닐 수 없다. 이 대통령의 당 대표 시절 사무총장을 지내고 대통령 정무특보를 역임한 조 의원은 언론 인터뷰에서 “의장은 중립이지만 여당 출신 의장으로서 정부와 손발을 맞춰야 한다”며 입법 과정의 협치보다 속도가 중요하다고 했다. 박 의원 역시 “야당이 위원장인 상임위가 회의를 안 열어 법안 통과가 안 된다”며 협치가 안 되면 단호하게 책임정치를 하겠다고 말했다. 21대 국회 원내대표로 민주당이 모든 상임위원장을 맡는 과정을 주도했던 김 의원 등은 여당의 상임위원장 독식도 배제하지 않는다는 입장이다. 심지어 박 의원은 필리버스터(무제한 토론) 규정까지 바꿔 국회의장의 권한을 강화하겠다는 입장이다. 국회법에 따라 국회의장은 의장으로 재직하는 동안 당적을 가질 수 없다. 국회의장의 정치적 중립성과 국회의 공정한 운영을 보장하려는 취지다. 2002년 국회법 개정 때도 이 조항 신설 이유를 “의장의 중립성을 보장하고 국회의 위상을 제고하기 위해서”라고 설명했었다. 과거 국회의장이 권한을 편파적으로 사용하면서 국회가 파행하거나 헌정 질서가 흔들린 경험을 반영한 것이었다. 지금 정치권의 대립은 어느 때보다 심각하다. 그런데도 국회의장을 맡겠다는 다선 의원들이 여당의 선봉대 역할을 자임하고 있어 개탄스럽다. 의장 후보들은 저마다 개헌안 처리를 공약하고 있지만, 타협이 사라진 국회에서 쟁점이 크지 않은 개헌안도 무산되는 것을 보지 않았나. 여야 간 갈등을 끈기 있게 중재하기는커녕 정파적으로 국회를 끌고 가겠다고 공언하는 이들이라면 국회의 수장이 될 자격이 없다.
editorial.pullQuote: 의장은 중립이지만 여당 출신 의장으로서 정부와 손발을 맞춰야 한다는 조 의원의 발언
summary: 민주당 국회의장 후보들이 여당 지원을 우선시하며 중립성을 외면하고 있다.
조정식·박지원·김태년 의원은 야당과의 협치보다 정부와의 손발 맞추기를 강조했다.
정치적 중립을 의무화한 국회법 취지를 무시하는 후보들은 의장직 자격이 없다.
editorial.stance: 국회의장 후보들은 정파적 이익보다 국회의 중립성과 민주주의 수호를 우선해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: [사설] 일방통행 불사한다는 국회의장 후보들 자격 없다
summaryTr: Democratic Party parliamentary speaker candidates are prioritizing ruling party support while turning a blind eye to neutrality.
Representatives Jo Jeong-sik, Park Ji-won, and Kim Tae-nyeon emphasized coordinating with the government over cooperation with the opposition.
Candidates who ignore the intent of the National Assembly Act, which mandates political neutrality, are unfit for the speaker position.
editorial.bodyTr: Three Democratic Party lawmakers—Rep. Jo Jung-sik (sixth term), Park Ji-won (fifth term), and Kim Tae-nyeon (fifth term)—have thrown their hats in the ring for the position of National Assembly speaker to lead the second half of the 22nd National Assembly over the next two years. The Democratic Party, which has traditionally selected its speaker candidate through voting by party members in the National Assembly, is now reducing the proportion of lawmaker votes to 80 percent and incorporating 20 percent of general party member votes. Given that the Democratic Party holds a majority of seats, winning the party primary essentially guarantees the speaker position. Party member voting has been underway since yesterday, and all three candidates are leading with pledges of support for the Lee government, relegating neutral National Assembly operations and cooperation with the opposition to the background. This is nothing short of ignoring the speaker's duties to cater to the preferences of the party's hardline members. Rep. Jo, who served as secretary-general during President Lee's tenure as party chair and as presidential political special adviser, said in a media interview that "the speaker should remain neutral, but as speaker from the ruling party, we must work in sync with the government," suggesting that speed in the legislative process is more important than compromise. Rep. Park similarly said that "opposition-held standing committees aren't holding sessions, preventing bills from passing," and vowed to take decisive responsibility politics if cooperation fails. Rep. Kim, who led the Democratic Party's move to hold all standing committee chair positions during the 21st National Assembly, does not rule out the ruling party monopolizing committee chairs. Park even indicated willingness to strengthen the speaker's authority by changing filibuster rules. According to the National Assembly Act, the speaker cannot maintain party affiliation during his tenure. This is intended to guarantee the speaker's political neutrality and ensure fair National Assembly operations. During the 2002 National Assembly Act revision, the provision was justified as being "to guarantee the speaker's neutrality and enhance the status of the National Assembly." This reflected past experiences in which National Assembly operations became derailed or constitutional order was shaken when speakers wielded their authority in a biased manner. Current political confrontation between ruling and opposition parties is more serious than ever. Yet it is lamentable that senior lawmakers seeking the speaker position are volunteering to serve as the vanguard for the ruling party. Though each speaker candidate pledges to handle constitutional revision, have we not witnessed even constitutional amendments of minor contention failing in a National Assembly devoid of compromise? Those who vow to lead the National Assembly along partisan lines rather than persistently mediating conflicts between ruling and opposition parties have no business being the head of the National Assembly.

editorial.title: Fired upon in Hormuz
editorial.body: Seoul must identify attacker, respond firmly, seek guarantees for safe passage The South Korean government said Sunday that the explosion and fire aboard the Namu, a cargo ship operated by South Korea’s HMM, in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4 were “caused by an external strike.” A Foreign Ministry spokesperson said, “The investigation confirmed that two unidentified airborne objects struck the stern of the HMM vessel at intervals of about one minute.” The two consecutive strikes strongly suggest that the attack was deliberate. Had the attack struck slightly lower, the vessel might have sunk. This is a shocking incident comparable to the 2010 sinking of the Cheonan by North Korea. The ministry said it remains unclear whether Iran was behind the attack, adding that investigators would conduct further analysis of debris recovered from the scene. The ministry summoned the Iranian ambassador to South Korea to its headquarters and briefed him on the investigation findings. Although the ministry stopped short of officially identifying the attacker, that the incident occurred in the inner Strait of Hormuz, an area within Iran’s sphere of influence, and that it summoned the Iranian ambassador after the investigation, strongly suggest Iran as being behind the attack. Speculation emerged early on that Iran was behind the attack, as it has previously carried out strikes in the region not only against US naval vessels, but also against ships from other countries. Shortly after the Namu incident, US President Donald Trump said that the incident was caused by an Iranian attack. However, a senior presidential official said, “The cause of the explosion is still unknown. It is not yet clear whether the vessel was struck.” The official added, “The matter first needs to be investigated, and that will take quite some time.” The South Korean government avoided the term "attack." We cannot but ask why it had to. It is questionable if intelligence was not shared. Iran’s state-run Press TV reported on Wednesday that a South Korean ship became a target after violating its newly established maritime regulations. The following day, however, the Iranian Embassy in Seoul dismissed the report, saying the article did not represent the official position of the Iranian government. In the end, the South Korean government’s investigation concluded that the incident was in fact caused by an attack. It also raises the possibility that Iran’s hard-line military, which reportedly operates on a different line from the Iranian government, may have carried out the attack upon its own initiative. Foreign Minister Cho Hyun said Monday that a closer examination is needed regarding the strike. While conducting a thorough investigation is the right approach in principle, the government should not drag the process out unnecessarily. Not only the South Korean public, but also the rest of the world is watching how the South Korean government responds. Now that it has been confirmed that the vessel was attacked, it is essential to determine whether the airborne objects were drones or missiles and, above all, who was responsible for the attack. The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people. The government must respond firmly. An issue involving a threat to public safety must not be brushed aside without a clear resolution. If further investigation confirms that Iran was behind the launch of the projectiles, the South Korean government should lodge a strong protest with Tehran and demand measures to prevent a recurrence. Given that South Korean vessels are still effectively trapped in the Persian Gulf, Seoul should obtain assurances from Iran that they will be able to pass through the strait safely. It should also consider participating actively in international coalition initiatives for Hormuz. Seoul condemns Hormuz vessel attack, but remains cautious on identifying culprit South Korea maintained a cautious stance Monday regarding the strike on a South Korean-operated vessel near the Strait of Hormuz, saying further analysis was needed before determining responsibility. editorial.pullQuote: The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people.
summary: South Korea confirmed a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz was hit by deliberate airborne strikes on May 4.
The government suspects Iran but avoids directly naming the attacker despite evidence pointing to Tehran.
Seoul must investigate fully, respond firmly, and secure safe passage guarantees for Korean vessels in the region.
editorial.stance: South Korea must stop hedging and demand Iran take responsibility for the attack while securing safe passage for its ships.
summaryTr: 한국이 5월 4일 호르무즈 해협의 화물선이 의도적인 공중 공격으로 피격됐음을 확인했다.
정부는 이란을 의심하고 있지만 테헤란을 가리키는 증거에도 불구하고 공격자를 직접 지목하지 않고 있다.
서울은 철저한 조사를 진행하고 강력하게 대응하며 이 지역에서 한국 선박의 안전한 항행을 보장받아야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr: 서울은 공격자를 규명하고 단호히 대응하며 안전한 통행 보장을 확보해야 한다 남한 정부는 지난 5월 4일 호르무즈 해협에서 한국 해운회사 HMM이 운영하는 화물선 나무호가 입은 폭발과 화재가 "외부의 타격으로 인한 것"이라고 일요일 밝혔다. 외교부 대변인은 "조사 결과 정체 불명의 비행물체 2개가 약 1분 간격으로 HMM 선박의 선미부를 타격한 것으로 확인됐다"고 말했다. 연속 두 차례의 타격은 공격이 의도적이었음을 강력하게 시사한다. 만약 공격이 조금 더 아래쪽을 맞혔다면 선박이 침몰했을 수도 있었다. 이는 2010년 북한의 천안함 침몰에 견줄 만한 충격적인 사건이다. 외교부는 이란이 공격의 배후인지 여부는 여전히 불명확하다고 말하면서 조사관들이 현장에서 회수한 파편을 추가로 분석할 것이라고 덧붙였다. 외교부는 이란 대사를 본부로 소환하여 조사 결과를 보고했다. 외교부가 공식적으로 공격자를 지목하지는 않았지만, 사건이 이란의 영향력 범위 내인 호르무즈 해협 내부에서 발생했다는 점과 조사 후 이란 대사를 소환했다는 점은 강하게 이란을 가리키고 있다. 이란이 과거 미국 해군함뿐 아니라 다른 국가의 선박들에 대해서도 이 지역에서 타격을 가해온 만큼, 일찍부터 이란이 배후일 가능성에 대한 추측이 나왔다. 나무호 사건 직후 도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령은 이 사건이 이란의 공격으로 인한 것이라고 말했다. 그러나 대통령실 고위 관계자는 "폭발의 원인은 여전히 불명확하다. 선박이 타격을 입었는지 여부도 아직 명확하지 않다"고 말했다. 관계자는 "먼저 조사가 필요하며, 이는 상당한 시간이 걸릴 것"이라고 덧붙였다. 남한 정부는 "공격"이라는 표현을 피했다. 우리는 왜 그럴 필요가 있었는지 묻지 않을 수 없다. 정보가 공유되지 않았던 것 아닌가 하는 의문이 제기된다. 이란 국영 언론사 프레스TV는 수요일 한국 선박이 새로 수립한 해양 규정을 위반한 후 공격 대상이 됐다고 보도했다. 하지만 그 다음날 서울의 이란 대사관은 이 기사가 이란 정부의 공식 입장을 대표하지 않는다며 이를 부인했다. 결국 남한 정부의 조사는 사건이 실제로 공격으로 인한 것이라는 결론에 도달했다. 이는 또한 이란 정부와 다른 노선에서 운영하는 것으로 알려진 이란의 강경파 군부가 자의적으로 공격을 감행했을 가능성도 제기한다. 조 현 외교부 장관은 월요일 이 타격에 대해 더 자세한 검토가 필요하다고 말했다. 철저한 조사를 실시하는 것은 원칙적으로 올바른 접근이지만, 정부는 불필요하게 과정을 질질 끌어서는 안 된다. 남한 국민뿐만 아니라 전 세계가 남한 정부의 대응 방식을 지켜보고 있다. 선박이 공격받았다는 것이 확인된 만큼, 비행물체가 드론인지 미사일인지 여부를 파악하고 무엇보다 공격의 책임자가 누구인지를 규명하는 것이 필수적이다. 나무호에 대한 공격은 남한 국민의 안전을 위협한 심각한 사건이다. 정부는 단호하게 대응해야 한다. 국민 안전에 대한 위협과 관련된 문제는 명확한 해결 없이 넘어가서는 안 된다. 추가 조사를 통해 이란이 발사체 발사의 배후임이 확인된다면, 남한 정부는 테헤란에 강력히 항의하고 재발 방지 조치를 요구해야 한다. 남한 선박들이 현재 실질적으로 페르시아만에 갇혀 있다는 점을 고려할 때, 서울은 해협을 안전하게 통행할 수 있도록 보장할 것을 이란에 요구해야 한다. 또한 호르무즈 해협의 안전을 위한 국제 연합 이니셔티브에 적극적으로 참여하는 것을 검토해야 한다.

editorials.title: Fired upon in Hormuz
editorials.body: Seoul must identify attacker, respond firmly, seek guarantees for safe passage The South Korean government said Sunday that the explosion and fire aboard the Namu, a cargo ship operated by South Korea’s HMM, in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4 were “caused by an external strike.” A Foreign Ministry spokesperson said, “The investigation confirmed that two unidentified airborne objects struck the stern of the HMM vessel at intervals of about one minute.” The two consecutive strikes strongly suggest that the attack was deliberate. Had the attack struck slightly lower, the vessel might have sunk. This is a shocking incident comparable to the 2010 sinking of the Cheonan by North Korea. The ministry said it remains unclear whether Iran was behind the attack, adding that investigators would conduct further analysis of debris recovered from the scene. The ministry summoned the Iranian ambassador to South Korea to its headquarters and briefed him on the investigation findings. Although the ministry stopped short of officially identifying the attacker, that the incident occurred in the inner Strait of Hormuz, an area within Iran’s sphere of influence, and that it summoned the Iranian ambassador after the investigation, strongly suggest Iran as being behind the attack. Speculation emerged early on that Iran was behind the attack, as it has previously carried out strikes in the region not only against US naval vessels, but also against ships from other countries. Shortly after the Namu incident, US President Donald Trump said that the incident was caused by an Iranian attack. However, a senior presidential official said, “The cause of the explosion is still unknown. It is not yet clear whether the vessel was struck.” The official added, “The matter first needs to be investigated, and that will take quite some time.” The South Korean government avoided the term "attack." We cannot but ask why it had to. It is questionable if intelligence was not shared. Iran’s state-run Press TV reported on Wednesday that a South Korean ship became a target after violating its newly established maritime regulations. The following day, however, the Iranian Embassy in Seoul dismissed the report, saying the article did not represent the official position of the Iranian government. In the end, the South Korean government’s investigation concluded that the incident was in fact caused by an attack. It also raises the possibility that Iran’s hard-line military, which reportedly operates on a different line from the Iranian government, may have carried out the attack upon its own initiative. Foreign Minister Cho Hyun said Monday that a closer examination is needed regarding the strike. While conducting a thorough investigation is the right approach in principle, the government should not drag the process out unnecessarily. Not only the South Korean public, but also the rest of the world is watching how the South Korean government responds. Now that it has been confirmed that the vessel was attacked, it is essential to determine whether the airborne objects were drones or missiles and, above all, who was responsible for the attack. The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people. The government must respond firmly. An issue involving a threat to public safety must not be brushed aside without a clear resolution. If further investigation confirms that Iran was behind the launch of the projectiles, the South Korean government should lodge a strong protest with Tehran and demand measures to prevent a recurrence. Given that South Korean vessels are still effectively trapped in the Persian Gulf, Seoul should obtain assurances from Iran that they will be able to pass through the strait safely. It should also consider participating actively in international coalition initiatives for Hormuz. Seoul condemns Hormuz vessel attack, but remains cautious on identifying culprit South Korea maintained a cautious stance Monday regarding the strike on a South Korean-operated vessel near the Strait of Hormuz, saying further analysis was needed before determining responsibility. editorials.pullQuote: The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people.
summary: South Korea confirmed a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz was hit by deliberate airborne strikes on May 4.
The government suspects Iran but avoids directly naming the attacker despite evidence pointing to Tehran.
Seoul must investigate fully, respond firmly, and secure safe passage guarantees for Korean vessels in the region.
editorials.stance: South Korea must stop hedging and demand Iran take responsibility for the attack while securing safe passage for its ships.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Dark Horse Race and Mudslinging in Superintendent Elections Demand Reform
summaryTr: The June 3 education superintendent election is proceeding amid voter apathy.
Educational policy competition has disappeared while conflicts between candidates are intensifying.
Urgent institutional reforms are needed to address the limitations of direct elections.
editorial.bodyTr: The June 3 nationwide superintendent of education elections are being held amid voter apathy and severe conflicts among candidates. Competition over educational policies for students, teachers, and parents has disappeared, and the process of unifying candidates has been marred by refusals to accept primary results and frequent lawsuits and complaints.
